title: Replace string whitelists with enums (SeaORM DeriveActiveEnum; schemars enum constraints in M1b)
description: Convert VALID_* string arrays (concern statuses/tags, goal statuses/comparisons, protocol kinds/verdicts, observation origins/kinds, plan item kinds/outcome statuses) to Rust enums via SeaORM DeriveActiveEnum (rs_type String, db_type Text — storage unchanged; kammerz precedent, e.g. entity/src/roll_event.rs). Entity Model fields become enum-typed; exhaustive match in briefing/services. Payoff in M1b: schemars derives JSON-Schema enum constraints on MCP inputs so the LLM sees allowed values in the tool schema. Skip strum unless VariantNames/Display needed for error messages. Ride the same type-tightening PR as healthie-8nb (chrono), before M1b plan is written. Per Steve 2026-07-17.

title: Switch timestamps to typed chrono (DateTime<Utc> for *_at, NaiveDate for *_on) + ADR
description: Override the glovebox/kammerz TEXT-as-String convention (pattern doc permits override with reason): type safety, real date math (briefing assembler currently parse_from_str's), serde still emits ISO-8601, SQLite storage stays TEXT via SeaORM with-chrono (already enabled). jiff preferred aesthetically but SeaORM 1.1 supports only chrono/time — revisit if SeaORM adds jiff. Do as immediate follow-up PR after PR #3 merges and BEFORE M1b freezes interfaces (greenfield, zero data = cheapest moment). Touches migration column types, entities, clock.rs, services, tests. Write ADR-0003 for the deviation.

title: M1a: healthie-shared domain library
description: Execute docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-16-m1a-healthie-shared.md — 11 TDD tasks: workspace scaffold, M1 schema migration, SeaORM entities, services (profile/concern/goal/protocol/observation/checkin/plan), briefing assembler. Done when Justfile ci is green and briefing::assemble is tested.

title: Healthie vision reset — personal-domain-pattern rebuild
description: Rebuild healthie greenfield per docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-16-healthie-vision-reset-design.md: Rust workspace (healthie-shared/-backend/-mcp + Svelte SPA), accountable AI checkin loop, HAE ingest, rules layer, documents, SPA. Milestones M1a-M5.

title: Svelte stores (health data + settings state)
description: Svelte 5 stores using runes ($state, $derived) for: parsed health data from Tauri backend, user settings (event date, thresholds, date range filters), and derived/filtered datasets that feed into chart components.

title: MetricCard component
description: Reusable card component displaying summary statistics for a single metric: current value, mean, min, max, trend arrow (up/down vs previous period), pre/post event change with percentage. Accepts data from Tauri analysis commands.

title: TimelineChart component (ECharts)
description: ECharts time-series scatter plot with configurable threshold lines (e.g. SpO2 94%/90%, HR 100bpm) and vertical event markers (illness date). Support zoom/brush for date range selection. Used for SpO2, HR, HRV, respiratory rate timeline views.

title: FileImport component with progress bar
description: File import component: drag-drop zone or native file picker (Tauri dialog) for selecting the Apple Health export.xml. Show file size, trigger parse command, display progress bar with live updates from Tauri events (records processed, rate, percentage).

title: Dashboard layout and settings panel
description: Main dashboard layout component. Responsive grid of MetricCards and charts. Settings panel for configuring illness/event date, date range filters, and SpO2/HR thresholds. Use Svelte 5 runes ($state, $derived) for reactive state management.

title: Frontend Dashboard (Svelte 5 + ECharts)
description: Svelte 5 frontend with ECharts visualizations. Single-page dashboard showing health metrics with interactive charts, date filtering, and illness event markers. Replaces the static matplotlib PNGs and text reports from the Python version.

title: SpO2 deep analysis (sleep patterns, desaturation detection)
description: Port deep_analysis.py SpO2 analysis: categorize readings (critical/low/borderline/normal), time-of-day distribution, sleep-hour desaturation detection, nightly min/mean stats, consecutive low reading patterns for sleep apnea indicators.

title: Basic statistics computation (per-metric)
description: Compute per-metric statistics: mean, median, min, max, std dev. Filter by date range (past year, past 30 days, custom). Group by time period (daily, weekly, monthly). Return as JSON for MetricCard components.

title: Pre/post event comparison analysis
description: Compare metrics before vs after a configurable event date (default: illness start). Use a 90-day pre-event baseline window. Compute absolute and percentage changes. Port logic from process_health.py lines 175-188.

title: Analysis Engine (Rust Backend)
description: Rust analysis functions that compute statistics, comparisons, and correlations from parsed health data. Exposed as Tauri commands returning JSON for the frontend to render. Ports the logic from both process_health.py and deep_analysis.py.

title: Tauri command: parse with progress events
description: Create Tauri command parse_health_export that accepts a file path, runs the streaming parser, and emits progress events (percentage, records processed, rate) back to the frontend via app.emit(). The Python version logs every 500k records — replicate this as real-time progress updates.

title: Health record type definitions and HK identifier mapping
description: Define Rust structs for all 14 health metrics (heart_rate, resting_heart_rate, walking_heart_rate, hrv, vo2_max, blood_oxygen, respiratory_rate, sleep_analysis, weight, body_fat, active_energy, exercise_time, steps, flights_climbed) plus Workout. Map HKQuantityTypeIdentifier/HKCategoryTypeIdentifier strings to enum variants. Derive Serialize for Tauri IPC.

title: Streaming XML parser with quick-xml
description: Implement streaming XML parser using quick-xml Reader. Process Record and Workout elements, parse dates with chrono, extract values. Use elem.clear()-equivalent pattern for memory efficiency on multi-GB files. Port the logic from process_health.py lines 76-128.

title: XML Parser (Rust Backend)
description: Rust backend that streams Apple Health export.xml using quick-xml, extracts health records and workouts into typed structs, and exposes parsed data to the frontend via Tauri commands. This is the performance-critical core of the app.

title: Frontend dependency setup (Svelte 5 + ECharts)
description: Set up Svelte 5 frontend with TypeScript, Vite, and ECharts for visualization. Configure SvelteKit or vanilla Svelte with Tauri integration. Add tailwindcss for styling.

title: Rust dependency setup
description: Configure Rust dependencies in Cargo.toml: quick-xml (streaming XML), polars (DataFrames), chrono + chrono-tz (datetime), serde/serde_json (serialization), clap (CLI for dev tooling), tauri (app framework). Set up workspace structure with src-tauri/.

title: Tauri + Svelte 5 project scaffold
description: Scaffold the Healthie Tauri + Svelte 5 project structure. Initialize Cargo workspace, Tauri config, Svelte 5 frontend with TypeScript, and configure build tooling (Vite). Remove or archive the original Python scripts.

title: Drop clippy allows missing_errors_doc/missing_panics_doc/must_use_candidate; write the docs
description: lib.rs inherited glovebox's allow set. Drop missing_errors_doc (write # Errors sections naming DomainError variants per service fn — doubles as source material for M1b tool descriptions) and missing_panics_doc (only honest panic is test_support::test_db). Try dropping must_use_candidate too; restore only if genuinely noisy. Ride the type-tightening PR with healthie-8nb + enums bead. Per Steve 2026-07-17.

title: M2: adopt chorez deploy kit for odroid
description: Atomic rollback-safe just deploy: dirty-tree guard incl. untracked, pre-deploy VACUUM INTO snapshot, upload to .new then stop/swap/start keeping .prev, 30s /api/health wait printing one-line rollback command, snapshot pruning. Hardened systemd unit (NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=strict, ReadWritePaths, crash-loop cap StartLimitIntervalSec=300/Burst=5). build.rs embeds git SHA surfaced at /api/health as deploy identity guard. Reference: chorez deploy/ + justfile; kammerz bootstrap recipe for first-time provisioning.

title: M1b: MCP bearer-token auth per fewd pattern
description: When healthie-mcp lands: middleware::from_fn_with_state resolving Authorization: Bearer via axum_extra TypedHeader, 256-bit opaque tokens argon2id-hashed at rest with constant-time verify + 8-char fingerprint, identity injected as typed request extension read via one grep-able helper, never log the token, MCP_ALLOWED_HOSTS host allowlist merged with rmcp localhost defaults (fewd server/src/mcp/ + services/mcp_token_service.rs is the reference). Single-user but required for Tailscale exposure.

title: Adopt three-formatter split: Prettier (frontend/e2e) + dprint (md/json/toml/yaml, pinned plugins) + rustfmt, behind just fmt/fmt-check
description: chorez convention: nothing formatted by two tools. dprint.jsonc excludes frontend/, e2e/, .beads/, CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md (bd-managed blocks); add .git-blame-ignore-revs for reformat commits. rustfmt.toml already landed in M1a.

title: Adopt CI workflow: all-checks aggregate, paths-filter, justfile-as-source-of-truth, aarch64 cross-check
description: Copy kammerz/chorez ci.yml shape: jobs delegate to just recipes; all-checks aggregate is the sole branch-protection gate (skipped=ok); dorny/paths-filter skips heavy jobs on docs/.beads-only PRs; Swatinem/rust-cache; pinned BUN_VERSION; aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu cargo check job so dep breaks surface in CI not mid-deploy. Land right after PR #3.

title: Enable PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON when real DB connections land (M1b/M2)
description: M1a migration declares FKs with ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL, but SQLite does not enforce them unless PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON is set per connection. Services self-enforce via require() so M1a behavior is correct, but cascade/set-null semantics silently won't fire. When healthie-mcp/backend create real connections (and in test_db), set the pragma — kammerz's db.rs shows the pattern (incl. FK-off during table-rebuild migrations).

title: Import-to-SQLite pipeline and cache detection
description: Write parsed records to SQLite during import. Batch inserts for performance. Detect duplicate imports via file hash. On app startup, load from SQLite instead of requiring re-parse. Expose Tauri commands for checking data freshness and triggering re-import.

title: SQLite schema and migration setup
description: Design and create SQLite schema for health records, workouts, user settings, and event markers. Use rusqlite or sqlx. Include migration support for schema changes. Store import metadata (file hash, record count, import date) to detect when re-parse is needed.

title: Data Persistence (SQLite)
description: Optional SQLite persistence layer so parsed health data survives app restarts without re-parsing the multi-GB XML every time. Store parsed records, user settings, and event markers.

title: ComparisonBar component (pre/post event)
description: Side-by-side bar chart comparing pre-event vs post-event metric averages. Show absolute and percentage change. Color-code improvements (green) vs regressions (red).

title: CorrelationPlot component (ECharts)
description: ECharts scatter plot for correlation visualization (HRV vs RHR, sleep SpO2 vs HRV). Show trend line and correlation coefficient. Support hover tooltips with date and values.

title: HourlyHeatmap / DayOfWeek chart component
description: ECharts bar/heatmap showing metric values by hour of day (SpO2 drops by hour) or by day of week (HRV weekly pattern). Highlight sleep hours and anomalous periods.

title: Heart rate pattern analysis
description: Port heart rate analysis: monthly resting HR trends, tachycardia episode detection (>100bpm during rest hours), trend line computation (linear regression).

title: Respiratory rate analysis
description: Port respiratory rate analysis: sleep vs awake rate comparison, pre/post illness change, elevated reading detection (>16 breaths/min).

title: HRV analysis and correlation engine
description: Port deep_analysis.py HRV analysis: day-of-week patterns, HRV-RHR correlation computation, lowest HRV day identification. Merge daily HRV and RHR data for scatter plot data.

title: In-memory data store for parsed records
description: Store parsed health data in memory (or optionally SQLite) so it persists across Tauri command invocations without re-parsing. Implement a data store that the analysis commands can query. Consider using polars DataFrames or a simple Vec<HealthRecord> grouped by metric type.

title: Dev tooling and CI config
description: Set up CI basics: .gitignore for Tauri/Rust/Node artifacts, editorconfig, rustfmt.toml, eslint/prettier for frontend. Ensure cargo build and npm run build both work.

title: Move clippy strictness into [workspace.lints] in root Cargo.toml
description: Gate flags (-D warnings -D clippy::pedantic) currently live only in Justfile/CI invocations, so rust-analyzer and plain cargo clippy don't see them. [workspace.lints] + lints.workspace=true in member crates makes the strictness ambient. From repo-hygiene unit's out-of-scope findings; needs Cargo.toml ownership (do after type-tightening PR merges to avoid churn).

title: Normalize TOML formatting + remove temporary dprint Cargo.toml/pyproject excludes after PR train merges
description: The repo-hygiene unit excludes **/Cargo.toml (toml plugin reorders keys — would conflict with parallel type-tightening edits) and pyproject.toml (deleted by PR #4) from dprint. After the current PR train (repo-hygiene, type-tightening, adr-docs-cleanup) fully merges: remove both excludes, run dprint fmt, land as a standalone mechanical-formatting PR (merge LAST per kammerz rule), record the commit in .git-blame-ignore-revs.
notes: Unit B finding (empirically verified): dprint toml plugin supports {"toml": {"cargo.applyConventions": false}} which formats Cargo.toml WITHOUT reordering keys — likely lets us remove the **/Cargo.toml exclude with zero merge churn instead of a repo-wide reorder. Try this first.

title: Design review: Goal shapes after ~4 real checkins
description: goal.rs currently covers metric-target (at-most/at-least/range) + qualitative. Anticipated shapes from real usage: habit/frequency (PT 3x/week), rolling-window maintenance (pain-free 90 days), milestone (complete PT program). Deliberately deferred per YAGNI — let real checkin data reveal which shapes occur, then extend additively (string-whitelist kinds = additive migrations). Revisit after ~4 real checkins have produced goals that don't fit.

title: Overhaul healthie CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md: thin-index + rules files + beads doctrine
description: Current CLAUDE.md has template placeholders. Adopt kammerz thin-index over .claude/rules/*.md @-imports (tech-stack, commands, architecture, backend-patterns, workflow); fewd verbatim beads doctrine (post-merge chore(beads) close commits, JSONL full-snapshot semantics); AGENTS.md non-interactive-shell rules (rm -f, ssh -o BatchMode=yes); chorez-style Security-invariants section once there are invariants to state. Do after M1b when real commands/architecture exist to document.

title: Seed docs/adr/ with kammerz ADR discipline
description: docs/adr/README.md conventions (kammerz): NNNN-kebab-title.md, Nygard Context/Decision/Consequences, immutable once Accepted, supersede-dont-edit, Related: block cites bead IDs. ADR-0001 = record-architecture-decisions; ADR-0002 distills the 2026-07-16 vision-reset spec decisions. THEN retire committed transient docs: git rm docs/superpowers/** (survives in history) and gitignore docs/superpowers/ going forward — per Steve 2026-07-16: superpowers specs/plans are transient working artifacts, never committed; durable decisions live in ADRs. Active plans may exist on disk untracked while driving execution.

title: Add ci-before-push guard hook + scripts/ci-check.sh
description: fewd pattern: .claude/hooks/ci-before-push.sh as PreToolUse(Bash) hook blocking git push / gh pr create unless just ci passes (exit 2), SKIP_CI_HOOK=1 escape hatch. scripts/ci-check.sh runs all gates in one pass showing every failure at once.

title: Remove legacy Python scaffold (main.py, process_health.py, deep_analysis.py, pyproject/uv)
description: Old-vision Python analysis scripts are superseded by the Rust rebuild (healthie-hf2). They carry 6 dependabot vulns (3 high) on the default branch. Remove once the Rust workspace is established and any wanted analysis ideas are captured; also closes the dependabot PR (pillow bump).

title: User settings persistence
description: Persist user preferences: event/illness date, SpO2/HR thresholds, preferred date ranges, dashboard layout choices. Load on app startup, save on change. Use a simple key-value table or a settings struct serialized to JSON.

title: CSV export command
description: Export parsed data to CSV files (one per metric + workouts.csv), matching the output format of the Python process_health.py script. Expose as a Tauri command so the user can save data for external analysis.

title: Consider git-native just-ci gate in pre-push hook chain
description: core.hooksPath resolves to .beads/hooks/ which already has a pre-push shim; adding a just-ci step there would also gate human terminal pushes, not just Claude-run ones (the .claude PreToolUse hook only covers the latter). Investigate whether bd's hook chain supports custom steps cleanly; weigh against annoyance for quick WIP pushes. From repo-hygiene unit's out-of-scope findings.

title: M5: e2e conventions + living TEST_PLAN.md/UI_DESIGN.md docs
description: chorez e2e shape: isolated e2e/ sub-package (own lockfile/prettier), port-scoped preflight kills (never pkill -f), bounded PID-liveness wait_for, e2e-one for single-test iteration, cfg(debug_assertions)-gated /api/__test/reset endpoint. kammerz living docs: route-by-route TEST_PLAN.md manual QA script + UI_DESIGN.md design system wired to component paths with auto-applied design-system skill.
